identity.name: tg_bot_history (Module)
identity.purpose: Object-oriented Python module for monitoring, parsing, and persisting Telegram interaction data into a structured SQLite database.

components.class: 1. Encapsulates SQLite operations and connection management. 2. Implements idempotent UPSERT patterns for all primary entities. 3. Manages database initialization and PRAGMA settings (WAL, Foreign Keys). 4. Provides high-level aggregation methods for reporting and exporting.

1. Primary interface for the application layer. 2. Manages an internal instance of DatabaseRepository. 3. Contains private methods for extracting metadata from aiogram objects. 4. Orchestrates the flow: Extract -> Transform -> Persist. 5. Provides initialize_storage() to abstract DB setup.

table.name: users
table.description: Unique Telegram users with profile metadata.
table.fields: id (PK), username, first_name, last_name, is_bot, language_code, updated_at.

table.name: chats
table.description: Telegram groups, supergroups, channels, and private chats.
table.fields: id (PK), type, title, username, description, updated_at.

table.name: messages
table.description: Log of captured messages with edit history support.
table.fields: id (PK), tg_id, chat_id (FK), sender_id, reply_to_local_id, forward_sender_id, forward_message_id, forward_sender_name, original_text, text, entities (JSON), media_group_id, date, edit_date.

table.name: message_media
table.description: Metadata for files attached to messages (metadata only).
table.fields: id (PK), message_id (FK), file_id, file_unique_id, file_type, file_size, mime_type, width, height.

table.name: chat_members
table.description: Intersection table for user status and activity within specific chats.
table.fields: chat_id (PK), user_id (PK), status, joined_at, left_at, first_activity, last_activity, updated_at.

principles.principle: Stateful Interaction: Path-based instantiation of the HistoryCollector class.
Encapsulation: DatabaseRepository handles its own connection lifecycle per method call.
Agnosticism: The module remains independent of the bot's access control lists (ACLs).

integration_logic.initialization_flow: 1. Instantiate collector = HistoryCollector(db_path). 2. Call collector.initialize_storage() to ensure schema integrity.
integration_logic.message_flow: 1. App layer (bot.py) validates the source against ACLs. 2. App layer calls collector.process_message(message). 3. Collector extracts metadata via private _extract_* methods. 4. Collector calls repository methods to upsert data.

operational_notes.note: Connection Safety: Every repository method opens and closes its own connection via a private _get_connection() helper, ensuring thread safety in a multi-handler environment.
WAL Mode: Enabled to support concurrent read operations by maintenance tools (dump_db.py) while the bot processes updates.
